text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic right hemicolectomy with extended DIII lymph node dissection using a caudal to cranial approach. Five trochars are positioned: the optical trochar below the umbilicus, operative trochars in the lower left and right quadrants, and two trochars in the right flank and epigastric region. The dissection begins by incising the peritoneum near the ileocecal junction, where the visceral peritoneum meets the intestinal mesentery.

The surgeon gently separates the mesocolon from the anterior surface of the duodenum at the head of the pancreas, carefully respecting the threaded fascia. A gauze is placed on the spleen as a landmark. A window is opened into the mesentery near the ileal resection line, and the last ileal artery is traced to the superior mesenteric artery.

The surgeon identifies and dissects the superior mesenteric artery and vein in a caudal to cranial direction up to the duodenum. The ileocolic vein and artery are skeletonized with a harmonic scalpel, clipped, and divided at their origin. The middle colic vessels are reached and divided, with the right branches of the middle colic artery and vein also clipped and divided.

The surgeon reaches the greater curvature of the stomach and cuts the right gastroepiploic artery and vein. The right colon is fully mobilized through a medial to lateral dissection between the Gerota's and Toldt's fascia. A single stitch is used to fix the last part of the ileum to the transverse colon.

A linear stapler is inserted through the service openings to perform a side-to-side intracorporeal anastomosis. The second stapler closes the service openings and divides the specimen, followed by additional sutures placed along the stapler line. The posterior vaginal fornix is opened, and a plastic sleeve is inserted into the pelvis for specimen extraction using a turning movement. The colpotomy is closed with a V-lock suture.
A drainage tube is placed into the abdominal cavity, and the final view of the abdomen is shown, indicating the completion of the procedure.
